The Susitna 100 is a 100-mile race from Big Lake out to the Iditarod Trail heading to Trail Lake, then Yentna River before returning to Big Lake. Cross-country skiers, mountain bike and runners-walkers compete. Mandatory survival gear must be carried by all competitors the entire distance. The Little Su 50-K is a shorter version of the 100-mile event, with racers going out the Iditarod Trail to the "Nome 1049" sign and returning, for a total of 31 miles. summary: | | ANWR reports called inflated CRITIQUE: Researchers say much less is likely to be recoverable. The Associated Press (Published: January 15, 2003) Fairbanks -- Major U.S. newspapers, broadcasters and magazines have generally overstated the amount of oil likely to be recoverable from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge, according to a group of government-funded researchers. The researchers surveyed 35 news reports or articles between December 2000 and September 2001 as Congress was debating whether to allow oil drilling in ANWR's coastal plain.
